messages.content: Create a circle, triangle and finally a rectangle. Add text at the top of the geometrical shape.

from manim import *

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Set the background color
        self.camera.background_color = "#131313"
        
        # Create our shapes with larger size
        # Create a circle
        circle = Circle(color=WHITE, radius=2)
        circle_text = Text("Let's start with a perfect circle", color=WHITE).scale(0.8)
        circle_text.to_edge(UP, buff=1)
        
        # Create a triangle
        triangle = Triangle(color=WHITE).scale(2)
        triangle_text = Text("Now, let's transform it into a triangle", color=WHITE).scale(0.8)
        triangle_text.to_edge(UP, buff=1)
        
        # Create a rectangle
        rectangle = Rectangle(color=WHITE, width=4, height=3)
        rectangle_text = Text("Finally, let's change it into a rectangle", color=WHITE).scale(0.8)
        rectangle_text.to_edge(UP, buff=1)

        # Position shapes at center
        for shape in [circle, triangle, rectangle]:
            shape.move_to(ORIGIN)

        # Animate them one by one with text
        self.play(
            Write(circle_text, run_time=1),
            Create(circle, run_time=2)
        )
        self.wait(1.5)

        self.play(
            ReplacementTransform(circle, triangle, run_time=1.5),
            FadeOut(circle_text, shift=UP),
            FadeIn(triangle_text, shift=UP)
        )
        self.wait(1.5)

        self.play(
            ReplacementTransform(triangle, rectangle, run_time=1.5),
            FadeOut(triangle_text, shift=UP),
            FadeIn(rectangle_text, shift=UP)
        )
        self.wait(2)
Start the frame from red square and transform it into a blue circle

from manim import *

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Create a red square
        square = Square(color=RED, fill_opacity=1)
        
        # Create a blue circle, but don't show it initially
        circle = Circle(color=BLUE, fill_opacity=1)
        
        # Position the circle at the square's position
        circle.move_to(square.get_center())
        
        # Play the transformation from the square to the circle
        self.play(Transform(square, circle))
        
        # Hold on to the final frame
        self.wait(2)
Show the text "Arte de programar", really big
Draw a circle and tell we're drawing a circle

from manim import *
from manim_voiceover import VoiceoverScene
from manim_voiceover.services.gtts import GTTSService
from manim.scene.scene_file_writer import SceneFileWriter

# Disable the creation of subcaption files only when using Text-to-Speech
SceneFileWriter.write_subcaption_file = lambda *args, **kwargs: None

class GenScene(VoiceoverScene):
    def construct(self):
        # Set the voiceover service to Google Text-to-Speech
        self.set_speech_service(GTTSService())
        
        # Create a circle of color BLUE
        circle = Circle(color=BLUE)

        # Voiceover with animation
        with self.voiceover(
            text="We are now drawing a circle.",
            subcaption="Drawing a circle"
        ) as tracker:
            self.play(Create(circle), run_time=tracker.duration)
Create a blue square with green borders

from manim import *

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        name = Tex("Richie").to_edge(UL, buff=0.5)
        sq = Square(side_length=0.5, fill_color=GREEN, fill_opacity=0.5).shift(LEFT*3)
        tri = Triangle().scale(0.5).to_edge(DR)
        self.play(Write(name))
        self.play(DrawBorderThenFill(sq), run_time=2)
        self.play(Create(tri))
        self.wait()

Create a bar chart of Beverage Consumption with Coffe as 85, Tea as 45, Water as 65, Soda as 30 and Juice as 55

from manim import *

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Set the background color to #131313
        self.camera.background_color = "#131313"

        # Create the data for the chart
        data = {"Coffee": 85, "Tea": 45, "Water": 65, "Soda": 30, "Juice": 55}
        
        # Create the axes
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 6, 1],
            y_range=[0, 100, 20],
            axis_config={"color": WHITE},
            x_length=7,
            y_length=5.5,
            tips=False
        )

        # Create title
        title = Text("Beverage Consumption", color=WHITE).to_edge(UP)

        # Create bars
        bars = VGroup()
        labels = VGroup()
        values = VGroup()
        
        colors = [BLUE, GREEN, YELLOW, RED, PURPLE]
        
        for i, (category, value) in enumerate(data.items()):
            # Create bar with more width space between them
            bar = Rectangle(
                height=value/20,
                width=0.7,
                fill_opacity=0.8,
                color=colors[i]
            ).move_to(axes.c2p(i+1.2, value/2))
            
            # Create label
            label = Text(category, color=WHITE, font_size=20).next_to(bar, DOWN, buff=0.3)
            
            # Create value
            value_text = Text(str(value), color=WHITE, font_size=24).next_to(bar, UP)
            
            bars.add(bar)
            labels.add(label)
            values.add(value_text)

        # Add everything to the scene
        self.play(Write(title))
        self.play(Create(axes))
        
        # Add bars with smooth animation
        for bar, label, value in zip(bars, labels, values):
            self.play(
                GrowFromEdge(bar, DOWN, rate_func=smooth),
                FadeIn(label),
                FadeIn(value),
                run_time=0.8
            )
        
        self.wait()
Draw a blue square, a red circle inside a box of three sides. Make the blue square be on top of the red circle

from manim import *
from manim_physics import *

class GenScene(SpaceScene):
    def construct(self):
        circle = Circle().shift(UP)
        circle.set_fill("#F43F5E", 1)
        circle.set_stroke("#F43F5E")
        circle.shift(DOWN + RIGHT)

        rect = Square().shift(UP)
        rect.rotate(PI / 4)
        rect.set_fill("#3B82F6", 1)
        rect.set_stroke("#3B82F6")
        rect.shift(UP * 2)
        rect.scale(0.5)

        # Make the container wider
        ground = Line([-6, -3.5, 0], [6, -3.5, 0])  # Extend the ground line
        wall1 = Line([-6, -3.5, 0], [-6, 3.5, 0])   # Move the left wall further left
        wall2 = Line([6, -3.5, 0], [6, 3.5, 0])     # Move the right wall further right
        walls = VGroup(ground, wall1, wall2)
        self.add(walls)

        self.play(
            DrawBorderThenFill(circle),
            DrawBorderThenFill(rect),
        )
        
        self.make_rigid_body(rect, circle)  # Mobjects will move with gravity
        self.make_static_body(walls)  # Mobjects will stay in place

        self.wait(10)  # Extend the wait time to 10 seconds

from manim import *

        self.play(FadeIn(text))

Show a blue square and transform it into a pentagon of color green

from manim import *

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Create a square with a specific color
        square = Square(color=BLUE)
        # Create a pentagon with a color
        pentagon = RegularPolygon(n=5, color=GREEN)
        
        # Position the pentagon over the square
        pentagon.move_to(square.get_center())
        
        # Display the square and then it into a pentagon
        self.play(Create(square))
        self.play(Transform(square, pentagon))
        self.wait(2)

Can you make a video explaining tangent lines and derivatives using a visual, color-coded approach like this graph? A blue parabola curves upward, while a red tangent line touches it at a specific point, showing the slope. A white vertical guide marks the x-value where the derivative is evaluated. As the tangent moves, the slope changes, illustrating differentiation dynamically. A step-by-step breakdown with animations would make this concept clear and engaging!

from manim import *

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Create axes
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-5, 5, 1], y_range=[-5, 10, 1],
            axis_config={"include_numbers": True, "color": GREY}
        )
        
        # Create the graph for y = x^2 + 1
        parabola = axes.plot(lambda x: x**2 + 1, x_range=[-3, 3], color=BLUE)
        
        # Create the graph for y = 4x - 3
        linear_graph = axes.plot(lambda x: 4*x - 3, x_range=[-3, 3], color=RED)
        linear_graph2 = axes.plot(lambda x: 1000*x-2000, x_range=[2, 2.005], color=WHITE)
        dashed_graph=DashedVMobject(linear_graph2)
        # Display the axes and graphs
        self.play(Create(axes))
        self.play(Create(parabola))
        self.play(Create(dashed_graph))
        self.play(Create(linear_graph))

Make a video with a linear regression and the line of best fit using visuals like a graph. Show how the red best-fit line represents the trend among white data points on a green-axis coordinate plane. Walk through calculating the line, interpreting the slope, and measuring accuracy

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Setting up the axes
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-10, 10, 1],
            y_range=[-10, 10, 1],
            axis_config={"color": GREEN}
        )
        
        # Adding labels for the axes
        x_label = axes.get_x_axis_label("X")
        y_label = axes.get_y_axis_label("Y")

        # Create data points
        dots = VGroup()
        np.random.seed(42)
        x_values = np.random.uniform(-5, 5, 10)
        y_values = 2 * x_values + np.random.normal(0, 1, 10)  # Some linear relation

        for x, y in zip(x_values, y_values):
            dot = Dot().move_to(axes.c2p(x, y))
            dots.add(dot)

        # Displaying axes and dots
        self.play(Create(axes), Write(x_label), Write(y_label))
        self.play(Create(dots))

        # Calculate and plot the regression line
        fit = np.polyfit(x_values, y_values, 1)
        regression_line = axes.plot(lambda x: fit[0]*x + fit[1], color=RED)

        # Display the regression line
        self.play(Create(regression_line))
        self.wait(2)
Create a 3D cube moving

from manim import *

class GenScene(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        # Create a 3D cube
        cube = Cube(side_length=2, fill_opacity=0.8)
        
        # Add the cube to the scene
        self.add(cube)
        
        # Set the camera orientation
        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=75 * DEGREES, theta=30 * DEGREES)
        
        # Rotate the cube
        self.begin_ambient_camera_rotation(rate=0.2)
        self.play(Rotate(cube, angle=2*PI, axis=RIGHT), run_time=5)
        self.wait(2)

Create the logo of Animo using three circles of #FB7185 one on top of another

Create an animation that visually demonstrates the Riemann sum approximation of the integral of the function x squared over the interval from 0 to 4. Plot the function with labeled axes and overlay green Riemann rectangles using left-endpoint sampling with a step size of 0.5. Display the summation formula above the plot, then dynamically refine the approximation by decreasing the step size step by step (0.25, then 0.1, then 0.05), making the rectangles thinner for a more precise estimate. Finally, transition the summation formula into the definite integral notation, showing how Riemann sums converge to integrals as the step size approaches zero, effectively illustrating the concept of integration in calculus.

from manim import *

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Define the function to be plotted
        func = lambda x: x**2

        # Create axes
        axes = Axes(x_range=[0, 4], y_range=[0, 16], axis_config={"include_tip": False})

        # Plot the function using the axes
        plot = axes.plot(func, color=BLUE)

        # Create labels
        labels = axes.get_axis_labels(x_label="x", y_label="f(x)")

        # Add the plot and labels to the scene
        self.play(Create(axes), Create(plot), Write(labels))

        # Create rectangles for Riemann approximation
        dx = 0.5
        rectangles = axes.get_riemann_rectangles(graph=plot, x_range=[0, 4], dx=dx, color=GREEN, input_sample_type='left')

        # Display the Riemann rectangles
        self.play(Create(rectangles))

        # Add the summation formula above the plot
        sum_formula = MathTex(r"A \approx \sum_{i=1}^{n} f(x_i^*) \Delta x")
        sum_formula.to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(sum_formula))

        # Animate the rectangles getting thinner to approximate the area
        for new_dx in [0.25, 0.1, 0.05]:
            new_rectangles = axes.get_riemann_rectangles(graph=plot, x_range=[0, 4], dx=new_dx, color=GREEN, input_sample_type='left')
            self.play(Transform(rectangles, new_rectangles), run_time=2)

        # Transition the summation formula to the integral notation
        integral_formula = MathTex(r"A = \int_{0}^{4} x^2 \, dx")
        integral_formula.to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Transform(sum_formula, integral_formula))
Create a 3D blue cube, but from the perspective that looks like a square and then moving it and then again like a square

Start with a blue circle, then convert it into a rounded square, then add a new rounded square around it but bigger. Then add three circles (green, red and yellow) inside the bigger rounded blue square. Then convert the circles into a rounded squares again. Then duplicate the three new squares, as well as the rounded blue bigger square and make it even more more bigger

from manim import *
from math import *

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Crear un círculo inicial
        c1 = Circle(color=BLUE)
        self.play(Create(c1))
        
        # Transformar el círculo en un cuadrado con esquinas redondeadas
        s1 = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.5, width=2, height=2, color=BLUE)
        self.play(Transform(c1, s1))
        
        # Lista de cuadros para agregar iterativamente
        squares = [s1]

        # Número de iteraciones
        iterations = 5
        
        # Factor de aceleración
        acceleration_factor = 2
        
        # Expandir iterativamente los cuadrados
        for i in range(iterations):
            new_squares = []
            time = 1 / acceleration_factor**i
            
            for square in squares:
                # Expandir el cuadrado actual
                self.play(square.animate.scale(1.2), run_time=time)

                # Crear tres nuevos círculos alrededor del cuadrado expandido
                for direction, color in zip([UP + LEFT, UP + RIGHT, DOWN], [GREEN, RED, YELLOW]):
                    c = Circle(color=color).move_to(square.get_center() + direction)
                    self.play(Create(c), run_time=time)
                    s = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.5, width=2, height=2, color=color).move_to(c)
                    self.play(Transform(c, s), run_time=time)
                    new_squares.append(s)
            
            squares += new_squares

Animate three fundamental physics equations: illustrate efficiency scaling by comparing system and particle properties, show Einstein’s field equations connecting spacetime curvature with energy, and present the Dirac equation describing quantum spinor behavior, using smooth transitions and color highlights for clarity.

from manim import *

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Define equations
        equation1 = MathTex(r"\frac{\eta_u}{N_p \eta_p} = \frac{m_p r_u^2}{M_u (\pi r_p)^2} \sim 1")
        equation2 = MathTex(r"R_{\mu \nu} - \frac{1}{2} R g_{\mu \nu} + \Lambda g_{\mu \nu} = 8 \pi G T_{\mu \nu}")
        equation3 = MathTex(r"i \hbar \gamma^\mu \partial_\mu \psi = m c \psi")

        # Set colors for specific parts
        for eq in [equation1, equation2, equation3]:
            eq.set_color_by_tex_to_color_map({
                "\\eta": TEAL, "\\pi": TEAL, "\\mu": TEAL, "\\nu": TEAL, "\\Lambda": TEAL, "G": TEAL,
                "2": YELLOW, "8": YELLOW
            })
            eq.scale(2)  # Double the font size

        # Sequence of animations
        self.play(Write(equation1))
        self.wait(4)
        self.play(FadeOut(equation1))
        self.wait(1)

        self.play(Write(equation2))
        self.wait(4)
        self.play(FadeOut(equation2))
        self.wait(1)

        self.play(Write(equation3))
        self.wait(4)
        self.play(FadeOut(equation3))

        # Ensure total run time is at least 25 seconds
        self.wait(7)

Start from a green bordered 3d cube, then make it small, then flip it by a couple of times. The input is a yellow arrow says "Input" on the bottom, the red arrow says "Output". The zoom out, create a blue cube from inside of the green cube. The red arrow and change it to blue arrow, and replace the "Output" with "Generator". Move it smoothly and then make a cool camera rotation.

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class FirstScene(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        # Configuración inicial de la cámara
        self.set_camera_orientation(phi=30 * DEGREES, gamma=15 * DEGREES, theta=-20 * DEGREES)

        # Crear el cubo
        cube = Cube(side_length=2)
        cube.set_fill(BLACK, opacity=1)
        cube.set_stroke(color="#69F0AE", width=4)
        cube.move_to(ORIGIN)

        # Crear flechas 2D (invertidas)
        arrow_in = Arrow(start=LEFT * 3.5, end=LEFT * 1.5, color="#FFE57F", buff=0)
        arrow_out = Arrow(start=RIGHT * 1.5, end=RIGHT * 3.5, color="#FF7F75", buff=0)

        # Crear textos para las flechas
        text_in = Text("Input", font="Inter", color="WHITE").scale(0.5)
        text_out = Text("Output", font="Inter", color="WHITE").scale(0.5)

        # Posicionar los textos (ajustados para las flechas invertidas)
        text_in.next_to(arrow_in, DOWN)
        text_out.next_to(arrow_out, DOWN)

        # Agrupar elementos
        group = VGroup(cube, arrow_in, arrow_out, text_in, text_out)

        # Animación de aparición y movimiento
        self.play(
            Create(cube.scale(2)),
            run_time=1,
            rate_func=smooth
        )
        self.play(
            cube.animate.scale(0.5),
            run_time=1,
            rate_func=smooth
        )
        self.play(Rotate(cube, angle=3*PI, axis=UP), run_time=2, rate_func=smooth)
        
        # Función para actualizar la rotación de la cámara
        def update_camera(mob, alpha):
            self.set_camera_orientation(gamma=interpolate(15, 0, alpha) * DEGREES, theta=interpolate(-20, -120, alpha) * DEGREES)
            
        # Crear y animar flechas y textos con paneo de cámara
        self.play(
            Create(arrow_in),
            Create(arrow_out),
            Write(text_in),
            Write(text_out),
            UpdateFromAlphaFunc(cube, update_camera),
            run_time=1.5,
            rate_func=smooth
        )

        # Animación final
        self.play(
            Rotate(group, angle=2*PI, axis=UP),
            group.animate.scale(0.8),
            run_time=3,
            rate_func=smooth
        )

        self.wait(1)

        # Crear nueva flecha celeste y texto "Generador"
        new_arrow_out = Arrow(start=RIGHT * 1.5, end=RIGHT * 3.5, color="#80D8FF", buff=0)  # Celeste
        new_text_out = Text("Generator", font="Inter", color="WHITE").scale(0.5)  # Cambiado a blanco
        new_text_out.next_to(new_arrow_out, DOWN)

        # Crear nuevo cubo
        new_cube = Cube(side_length=1)
        new_cube.set_fill(BLACK, opacity=0.5)
        new_cube.set_stroke(color="#80D8FF", width=2)
        new_cube.move_to(cube.get_center())

        # Animación para reemplazar la flecha y el texto, y crear y mover el nuevo cubo
        self.play(
            ReplacementTransform(arrow_out, new_arrow_out),
            ReplacementTransform(text_out, new_text_out),
            GrowFromCenter(new_cube),
            new_cube.animate.shift(RIGHT * 0.5),
            run_time=2
        )
        
        # Mueve el cubo mucho mas a la derecha
        self.play(
            new_cube.animate.shift(RIGHT * 5),
            run_time=3,
            rate_func=smooth
        )

        # Actualizar el grupo con los nuevos elementos
        group.remove(arrow_out, text_out)
        group.add(new_arrow_out, new_text_out, new_cube)

        # Mover la cámara más a la derecha
        self.move_camera(
            phi=30 * DEGREES,
            theta=-110 * DEGREES,
            gamma=0,
            run_time=2
        )

        # Crear un ValueTracker para controlar la animación de la cámara
        camera_tracker = ValueTracker(0)

        # Definir una función para actualizar la cámara
        def update_camera(mob, alpha):
            t = camera_tracker.get_value()
            self.set_camera_orientation(
                phi=30 * DEGREES,
                theta=-110 * DEGREES,
                gamma=0,
                focal_distance=interpolate(self.camera.focal_distance, self.camera.focal_distance / 4, t),
                frame_center=interpolate(self.camera.frame_center, new_text_out.get_center(), t)
            )
Create a red circle and then bigger white-border circle. Then add two yellows that goes on that white border circle.

from manim import *
from math import cos, sin, pi

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Create the nucleus
        nucleus = Circle(radius=0.2, color=RED, fill_opacity=1)
        
        # Create electrons
        electron1 = Dot(point=[1, 0, 0], color=YELLOW)
        electron2 = Dot(point=[-1, 0, 0], color=YELLOW)
        
        # Define the orbit paths
        orbit1 = Circle(radius=1, color=WHITE)
        
        # Add nucleus and orbit paths to the scene
        self.play(Create(nucleus), Create(orbit1))
        
        # Add electrons to the scene
        self.play(Create(electron1), Create(electron2))

        # Animations for electrons to move in orbit
        def get_electron1_position(t):
            return np.array([cos(t*PI), sin(t*PI), 0])
        
        def get_electron2_position(t):
            return np.array([-cos(t*PI), -sin(t*PI), 0])
        
        # Apply the animations
        self.play(
            UpdateFromAlphaFunc(electron1, lambda mob, alpha: mob.move_to(get_electron1_position(alpha))),
            UpdateFromAlphaFunc(electron2, lambda mob, alpha: mob.move_to(get_electron2_position(alpha))),
            run_time=4, rate_func=linear, loop=True
        )

Create a white filled heart

from manim import *
from numpy import sin, cos, pi

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Parámetros de la curva de corazón
        heart = VMobject()
        heart_points = [
            self.heart_curve(t) for t in np.linspace(0, 2 * pi, 100)
        ]
        heart.set_points_as_corners(heart_points)
        heart.set_fill(WHITE, opacity=1)
        heart.set_stroke(WHITE, width=4)

        self.play(Create(heart))
        self.play(heart.animate.scale(1.2), rate_func=there_and_back, run_time=2)
        
    def heart_curve(self, t):
        x = 16 * sin(t)**3
        y = 13 * cos(t) - 5 * cos(2*t) - 2 * cos(3*t) - cos(4*t)
        return np.array([x, y, 0]) * 0.1
Show "Welcome to Astronware", create a blue circle around it, and make both small

from manim import *

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Create and display the text
        text = Text("Welcome to Astronware")
        self.play(Write(text))
        
        # Create a circle surrounding the text after text is created
        circle = Circle(color=BLUE)
        circle.surround(text)
        self.play(Create(circle))
        
        # Scale down the text and circle together to 0.5x their size
        group = VGroup(text, circle)
        self.play(group.animate.scale(0.5))
Create a bar chart with the values A, N, I, M, all increasing one after the other

from manim import *

class GenScene(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Data for the bar chart
        models = ["A", "N", "I", "M"]
        performances = [4, 6, 7, 11]
        colors = ["#FCD34D", "#FDA4AF", "#93C5FD", "#86EFAC"]

        # Create axes
        y_axis = NumberLine(
            x_range=[0, 12, 2], length=4.5, rotation=90 * DEGREES, include_tip=True,
            tip_width=0.25, tip_height=0.25
        ).shift(LEFT * 3.3 + DOWN * 0.5).set_color(WHITE)
        
        x_axis = NumberLine(
            x_range=[-0.5, 3.5, 1], length=7, include_tip=True,
            tip_width=0.25, tip_height=0.25
        ).shift(2.2 * DOWN).set_color(WHITE)
        
        y_axis_label = Text("Performance", color=WHITE).scale(0.5).rotate(90 * DEGREES).next_to(y_axis, LEFT, buff=0.5)
        x_axis_label = Text("Models", color=WHITE).scale(0.5).next_to(x_axis, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        
        # Position axes
        self.add(x_axis, y_axis, x_axis_label, y_axis_label)

        bars = []
        for i, (model, performance, color) in enumerate(zip(models, performances, colors)):
            bar = Rectangle(height=performance * 0.3, width=0.3, color=color, fill_opacity=1)
            bar.move_to(x_axis.n2p(i) + UP * performance * 0.15 + UP * 0.3)
            label = Text(model, color=WHITE).scale(0.5).next_to(bar, DOWN, buff=0.5)
            bars.append((bar, label, performance))

        # Animation
        for i, (bar, label, performance) in enumerate(bars):
            self.add(label)  # Add labels first, below the columns
            self.play(GrowFromEdge(bar, edge=DOWN), FadeIn(label), run_time=1, lag_ratio=0.1)

        self.wait(4)
